title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: There is no $\varepsilon > 0$ for which there is an algorithm for testing isomorphism of maps of unrestricted genus in time $O(n^{2-\varepsilon})$.
context_text: The best known algorithm for the simultaneous conjugation problem runs in time $O(n^2 \log d / \log n + dn \log n)$, yielding an $O(n^2/\log n)$ algorithm for map isomorphism and automorphism problems of unrestricted genus. In complexity theory this is not considered 'truly subquadratic', which motivates the conjecture.
paper_title: Automorphism groups of maps in linear time

title: Open subproblem: conditional superlinear lower bound
statement_text: An interesting open subproblem is to prove a conditional ``truly superlinear'' lower bound for any of the mentioned problems (map isomorphism, simultaneous conjugation).
context_text: Some progress has been made: the communication complexity of the simultaneous conjugation problem is $\Omega(dn\log n)$ for $d>1$, and under the decision tree model the search version has lower bound $\Omega(n\log n)$. A conditional lower bound ruling out, e.g., $O(n\,\mathrm{polylog}\,n)$ algorithms remains open.
notes: Stated in prose without a labelled environment; full paper text is truncated so further items in later sections may be missing.
paper_title: Automorphism groups of maps in linear time
